current.creator: Bob Dylan
current.title: Stuck Inside of Mobile with the Memphis Blues Again
current.quote:
It all seems so well timed

An’ here I sit so patiently

Waiting to find out what price

You have to pay to get out of

Going through all these things twice
current.reference: Bob Dylan, “Stuck Inside of Mobile with the Memphis Blues Again,” 1966. Written by Bob Dylan.
current.comment: I read the lines as a warning about timing because futures work often succeeds or fails in relation to a moment: when a scenario, warning, or intervention arrives, who is ready to hear it, and what institutions can still do with it. Dylan’s speaker notices that everything seems “so well timed,” but the cost appears when one assumes that the same passage can be repeated and escaped in the same way. In futures studies, this means a well-timed success should not become a formula: contexts shift, consequences accumulate, and going through the same things twice may carry a different price.
current.commentTitle: Why I read this as futures-studies wisdom
